session.lock_desc: Clear decrypted secrets from memory and return to the login screen. Your encrypted vault and sync providers stay on this device.
session.expired_idle: Session expired — unlock again to continue.
session.idle_warning: Locking in 30 seconds due to inactivity…

onboarding.intro_description: Your credentials are encrypted locally; only your approved devices can unlock them. To start, choose where to store your vault.
onboarding.local_description: Encrypted vault stays in browser storage on this device.
onboarding.github_description: Store and sync your encrypted vault securely in a private or public GitHub repository.
onboarding.another_provider: Another encrypted vault file on a different provider.

login.connect_prompt: Connect to storage, then get access.
login.scan_qr: Or scan enrollment QR code from an active device
login.join_request_sent: Join request sent. Please approve on an active device.
login.finish_onboarding_desc: Enter your vault password to finalize enrollment and generate keys.
login.qr_or_link: Scan QR or paste onboarding link
login.join_instructions: On a device that is already unlocked, open Onboard Device to get a QR code and onboarding link. Scan the QR here, or paste the full link below — then enter the vault password to finish.
login.enroll_qr_desc: Use this if you copied the link from the other device instead of scanning the QR.
login.paste_desc: Paste the full URL from the issuing device. It contains everything the QR encodes.
login.password_placeholder_qr: Vault password for this onboarding QR
login.onboarding_card_desc: This device scanned an onboarding QR. Enter the vault password to pull the vault from the sync provider into local storage and unlock.
login.password_entry_placeholder: Password for the vault entry shown in the QR
login.password_help_text: The password is not stored in the QR. Provider credentials are applied automatically after you enter it.
login.create_vault_subtitle: Create a vault in this browser, or connect a sync provider to open one you already have.
login.path_local_description: Create a vault secured by this browser's device keys. It lives here first — add sync providers in Settings anytime to replicate it.
login.path_cloud_description: Connect GitHub or another provider to open a vault that already exists, or to join a shared vault.
login.open_vault_subtitle: This browser can hold multiple vaults. Unlock one below or start fresh.
login.unlock_section_description: Use this device's keys or a backup password for the vault above.
login.other_vaults_description: Each vault is independent — its own secrets and sync providers.
login.create_vault_intro: This browser can hold more than one vault. Choose how to create or open your first one.
login.vault_picker_subtitle: Open an existing vault on this device, create another, or import one from a sync provider.
login.vault_picker_hint: Each vault has its own secrets and sync providers.
login.vault_name_hint: A short label to tell vaults apart on this device. The technical store id stays in small print below.
login.vault_name_required: Enter a name for your vault.
login.password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
login.local_vault_description: Unlock your vault with device keys or a backup password.
login.sync_after_unlock: Cloud sync providers can be connected in Settings after you unlock.
login.use_storage_provider: Use a cloud storage provider instead

local_only_warning.title: No backup — your vault exists only on this device
local_only_warning.body: Your encrypted vault is stored only in this browser. If this copy is lost, your secrets may be gone permanently. Add at least one sync provider to keep an encrypted replica elsewhere.
local_only_warning.risk_cleared_data: Clearing browser data, cookies, or site storage can delete your vault.
local_only_warning.risk_lost_device: A lost, replaced, or reformatted device has no other copy unless you add a sync provider.
local_only_warning.risk_no_guarantee: Nook does not host, back up, or recover vault data — there are no guarantees at this stage.

vault.switcher_one_on_device: One vault on this device — create another anytime.
vault.admin_vaults_desc: Create, rename, and switch local vaults on this device.

settings.title: Settings
settings.storage_desc: Replicate your vault to sync providers
settings.passwords_desc: Passwords available for unlock and device onboarding
settings.devices_desc: Enrolled browsers and pending access requests
settings.remove_provider_confirm: Are you sure you want to remove this provider?
settings.qr_instructions: Scan this code on another device to enroll it automatically into this vault.
settings.password_setup_desc: Set a vault password to allow unlocking without device keys.

help.title: Your vault, optional sync replicas
help.subtitle: One encrypted vault on this device — sync providers keep copies in sync.

local-first.title: Your vault
local-first.summary: A vault is one encrypted database. It always starts in this browser; sync providers are optional replicas — not separate vaults.
local-first.bullet1: Create a vault here, then add GitHub or Drive in Settings to replicate it.
local-first.bullet2: Unlock with device keys (default) or a backup password from Settings.
local-first.bullet3: Secrets are encrypted before they are saved or synced anywhere.
local-first.bullet4: No Nook account or central server holds your data.

unlock.title: Unlocking the vault
unlock.summary: The master password decrypts the vault. Device keys remain for quick unlock and multi-device approval.
unlock.bullet1: Create a master password when you set up a new vault.
unlock.bullet2: Optional backup passwords add recovery options and power onboarding QR codes.
unlock.bullet3: Each browser can keep its own device key for passwordless unlock after approval.
unlock.bullet4: Losing every password and every approved device means the vault cannot be recovered.

sync.title: Sync providers
sync.summary: Connect GitHub or Google Drive to push and pull immutable vault events — credentials move ciphertext only.
sync.bullet1: Add sync providers in Settings. Local storage stays the canonical copy.
sync.bullet2: Use Sync now or Sync all to reconcile with a provider manually.
sync.bullet3: After you save a secret, Nook fans out to every connected provider in the background.
sync.bullet4: A provider PAT or OAuth token can read/write the encrypted file — it cannot decrypt secrets.

conflicts.title: Sync conflicts
conflicts.summary: Event-log vaults merge by appending immutable events.
conflicts.bullet1: New vaults use an append-only event log — concurrent edits on different devices usually merge automatically.
conflicts.bullet2: The local YAML projection cache stays in this browser; providers sync event files under nook-log/v1/events/.
conflicts.bullet3: When concurrent replacements touch the same secret, Nook keeps the candidates until you choose the version to keep.
conflicts.bullet4: After resolution, sync continues normally to all providers.

onboard.title: Onboard another browser
onboard.summary: Generate a QR code that points a new device at a sync provider copy, then share the vault password separately.
onboard.bullet1: Pick a connected sync provider (for example GitHub) on the Onboard tab.
onboard.bullet2: Confirm a vault password entry — the QR encrypts provider access, not the password itself.
onboard.bullet3: The new browser downloads the vault from the provider into its local cache, then unlocks with the password you share.
onboard.bullet4: Onboarding skips the manual join-approval round trip when a password envelope is used.

join.title: Adding another device (approval flow)
join.summary: Without a QR code, a new browser sends a join request that an approved device must accept.
join.bullet1: Open Nook in the new browser and connect to the same sync provider.
join.bullet2: Review the pending join on a device that already has access.
join.bullet3: Approve it — the new browser receives vault keys and can unlock.

technical.title: Technical details
technical.summary: Security-sensitive work runs in a Rust core compiled to WebAssembly inside your browser.
technical.bullet1: Each browser creates a public/private keypair; private keys never leave the device.
technical.bullet2: Secrets are typed YAML records encrypted independently with age.
technical.bullet3: Sync providers store pretty-printed YAML event files, not plaintext secrets or mutable vault snapshots.
technical.bullet4: Decrypted secrets exist only in the active browser session.

device_protection.title: Protect this browser
device_protection.setup_description: Create a passkey to protect this browser's private device key.
device_protection.migration_description: Your existing device key needs a one-time security upgrade. Create a passkey to encrypt it without changing vault access.
device_protection.unlock_description: Authorize with your passkey before Nook can use this browser's private device key.
device_protection.pin_setup_description: This platform did not provide WebAuthn PRF. Set a local PIN to encrypt this browser's private device key instead.
device_protection.pin_unlock_description: Enter this browser's PIN before Nook can use the private device key stored here.
device_protection.unavailable_description: Device-key protection could not be initialized. Refresh and try again in a supported browser.
device_protection.existing_passkey_hint: Your browser or operating system will show saved Nook passkeys if any are available.
device_protection.mode_standard_description: Passkey PRF can recreate this browser's device identity if local browser storage is cleared.
device_protection.mode_anti_hacker_description: Passkey PRF unwraps a local device key. Clearing browser storage can permanently remove this device's access.
device_protection.pin_fallback_ready: This platform does not support WebAuthn PRF. Use a local PIN to protect this browser's key.
device_protection.recovery_pin_fallback_ready: This passkey cannot recover the existing device identity because WebAuthn PRF is unavailable. Set a local PIN to protect a new device identity instead.
device_protection.pin_security_note: Use at least 6 characters. A weak PIN can be guessed offline if someone copies this browser storage.
device_protection.recovery_warning: If this passkey is unavailable, reset this browser's device identity. Saved sync connections will be removed; local encrypted vaults remain.
device_protection.pin_recovery_warning: If this PIN is forgotten, reset this browser's device identity. Saved sync connections will be removed; local encrypted vaults remain.
device_protection.recovery_confirm: Reset this browser's device identity? Saved sync connections will be removed. Local encrypted vaults will remain, but you may need a vault password or another enrolled device to regain access.
device_protection.recovery_complete: Device identity reset. Create a new passkey to continue.

architecture_modes.device_mode_standard_description: Derive this browser's device key from the passkey. Convenient, and recoverable if browser storage is cleared.
architecture_modes.device_mode_anti-hacker_description: Keep a wrapped device key in this browser only. Clearing site data can permanently lose local vault access.
architecture_modes.vault_type_simple_description: Each enrolled device can open the vault with its own encrypted key envelope.
architecture_modes.vault_type_nexus_description: Multiple devices must be onboarded before the vault can create secrets.
architecture_modes.replication_type_personal_description: Trusted same-owner devices may receive sync-provider credentials during onboarding.
architecture_modes.replication_type_shared_description: Joiners use their own provider account; the owner grants shared storage access.
architecture_modes.onboarding_type_personal-credential-tran: Trusted devices receive the selected provider credentials inside the encrypted onboarding payload.
architecture_modes.onboarding_type_shared-provider-grant_de: The joiner signs in with their own provider identity after the owner grants access to shared storage.
architecture_modes.provider_capability_description: The next provider screen asks Rust which providers support this replication mode and explains unavailable choices.
architecture_modes.shared_grant_manual_instructions: Share the Drive folder “{folder}” with {email}, then have that person sign in with their own Google account before opening the onboarding link.
architecture_modes.shared_grant_success: Shared Drive folder “{folder}” is ready for {email}. They sign in with their own Google account, then open the onboarding link.
architecture_modes.shared_grant_unsupported: This provider cannot grant shared storage access.
architecture_modes.nexus_gate_description: Secret creation stays blocked until the required participants and encrypted shares are ready.
architecture_modes.nexus_secret_creation_blocked: Nexus secret creation is locked until the required encrypted key shares are present.
architecture_modes.nexus_ceremony_instructions: Open this device's share contribution, exchange it with another enrolled device, then unlock with both contributions. Treat contributions like a recovery code — copy once and do not store them in logs.
architecture_modes.nexus_ceremony_awaiting_shares: Waiting for enough enrolled devices before encrypted shares are issued. Approve additional devices, then return here.
architecture_modes.nexus_ceremony_copy_failed: Could not copy the share contribution.
architecture_modes.nexus_ceremony_open_failed: Could not open this device's nexus share.
architecture_modes.nexus_ceremony_paste_peer_placeholder: Paste one opened-share JSON per line
architecture_modes.nexus_ceremony_need_peers: Paste at least one peer share contribution to unlock.
architecture_modes.nexus_password_forbidden: Password unlock is forbidden for nexus vaults. Use the opened-share ceremony instead.

validation.github_pat_empty: Enter a GitHub personal access token to connect.
validation.oauth_access_token_empty: Sign in with Google to connect Google Drive.
validation.shared_joiner_identity_required: Enter the joiner provider identity before issuing an onboarding code.
validation.shared_joiner_identity_invalid: Enter a valid joiner email address.
validation.shared_storage_target_required: Create or select a shared storage folder before issuing an onboarding code.
validation.nexus_vault_has_full_key_envelopes: Nexus vaults cannot contain full per-device vault-key envelopes.
validation.simple_vault_has_nexus_shares: Simple vaults cannot contain nexus share records.
validation.invalid_nexus_share_set: The nexus share set is incomplete or inconsistent with the vault policy.
validation.github_repo_length: GitHub repository name must be 100 characters or fewer.
validation.github_repo_chars: Repository name may only contain letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, and underscores.
validation.drive_file_name_length: Drive vault file name must be 100 characters or fewer.
validation.drive_file_name_chars: Drive vault file name may only contain letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, and underscores.
validation.secret_id_reserved: Secret id cannot use a reserved device id.

errors.google_sign_in_required: Sign in with Google before unlocking.

device_protection.authorization_required: Authorize before using this browser's device key.

vault.decryption_failed: Decryption failed. Please check your password.
vault.connection_failed: Failed to connect to storage provider.

vault_passwords.title: Onboard another device
vault_passwords.desc: Generate a QR/link that carries provider access and a vault password so another browser can join this vault.
vault_passwords.warning_banner: Create a vault password to unlock from another browser or generate an onboarding QR from the Onboard page. Use a long, unique value.
vault_passwords.info_desc: These passwords can unlock the vault and can be selected on the Onboard page when you generate a QR/link for another device.
vault_passwords.label: Label
vault_passwords.remove_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this password? Enrolled devices using this password will not be affected, but you won't be able to onboard new devices with it.
vault_passwords.min_length_error: Password must be at least 5 characters.
vault_passwords.enter_pw_error: Enter the password for this entry to issue a code.
vault_passwords.remove_body_suffix: ? Other passwords stay active. If this is the last password, the vault returns to device-key unlock for this browser.
vault_passwords.issue_desc_suffix: to generate a QR/link for the new device.
vault_passwords.scan_qr_desc: Scan this QR with the new device to open Nook, or copy the onboarding link.
vault_passwords.link_desc: Open this URL on the new device's browser, or send it through a secure channel.
vault_passwords.share_password: Share the vault password separately — it is not included in the link.

onboard_device.title: Onboard Device
onboard_device.desc: Generate a QR that points a new browser at a sync provider copy of this vault. The new device downloads the vault locally, then unlocks with the vault password you share separately.
onboard_device.no_sync_providers: No sync providers connected. Add GitHub or Drive in Settings first.
onboard_device.choose_and_verify: Choose a password entry and verify it below.
onboard_device.shared_identity_hint: The joiner uses this provider identity for shared storage access; your provider credential is not placed in the code.
onboard_device.ready_desc: Scan this QR from the login screen on your other device, or use the onboarding link below.
onboard_device.link_desc: Open this URL on the new device's browser, or send it through a secure channel.
onboard_device.share_password: Share the vault password separately — it is not included in the link.
onboard_device.password_required_desc: Create a vault password before you can onboard another device. The new device will use this password to unlock the vault after downloading it from your sync provider.
onboard_device.wizard_password_subtitle: Create a password the new device will use to unlock
onboard_device.wizard_password_choose_singular: Choose which password to use for onboarding
onboard_device.wizard_password_existing_desc: Choose the vault password this onboarding link will use. You will re-type it before generating the link.
onboard_device.wizard_sync_subtitle: Connect GitHub or Drive so the new device can download the vault
onboard_device.no_compatible_sync_providers: No connected provider supports this vault's replication mode. Add a compatible provider first.
onboard_device.wizard_sync_empty_desc: Onboarding needs a cloud sync destination. Connect GitHub or Drive to host an encrypted copy of your vault.
onboard_device.wizard_generate_subtitle_ready: Verify your password and create the onboarding link
onboard_device.wizard_generate_subtitle_locked: Available after vault password and sync provider
onboard_device.missing_password_hint: Create a vault password before onboarding another device.
onboard_device.nexus_desc: Nexus devices join through the shared event log and participant approval. Password onboarding and QR links are not used.
onboard_device.nexus_no_password_desc: Each participant keeps its own device key. Connect the new browser to a compatible provider, then approve its join request from an existing participant.
onboard_device.nexus_step_connect: On the new browser, connect to a compatible provider that contains this vault's event log.
onboard_device.nexus_step_approve: Return to an existing participant, review the pending join request, and approve it to issue the participant share.
onboard_device.nexus_provider_ready: {count} compatible provider connections are available on this browser.
onboard_device.nexus_provider_missing: Connect a compatible provider before asking the new browser to join.

auth_storage.configure_desc: Configure where your encrypted vault file resides.
auth_storage.no_providers_saved: No providers saved. Create or connect a vault below.
auth_storage.confirm_remove: Remove "{label}" from saved providers?{signedOutNote} Your vault file on storage is not deleted.
auth_storage.signed_out_note: You will be signed out of the vault in this browser.
auth_storage.setup_desc: Connect and save this provider in this browser. Only the active provider is used for sync until you switch.
auth_storage.choose_desc: Pick where to store another encrypted vault file. Each provider can point at a different vault.
auth_storage.switch_providers_desc: Switch providers without changing device keys or backup passwords.
auth_storage.sync_providers_desc: Your vault always starts in this browser. Connect providers to keep encrypted replicas in sync.
auth_storage.sync_choose_desc: Pick a provider to replicate your vault. This browser remains the working copy.
auth_storage.sync_setup_desc: Connect this provider and reconcile with your vault using version sync.
auth_storage.sync_up_to_date: Already up to date with this provider.
auth_storage.sync_conflict: Versions match but content differs — choose which copy to keep.
auth_storage.sync_conflict_desc: This device and {provider} both report version {version}, but the encrypted content differs.
auth_storage.sync_conflict_banner: Sync conflict with {provider} — resolve before editing secrets.
auth_storage.sync_conflict_store_id_desc: This device ({localStore}) and {provider} ({remoteStore}) hold different vaults. Choose which one to keep — the other copy will be replaced.
auth_storage.sync_conflict_store_id_event_desc: This device ({localStore}) and {provider} ({remoteStore}) are different vaults. Provider event logs are append-only, so Nook will not merge or overwrite them automatically. Import the provider vault as another local vault, or choose a dedicated empty provider target for this device.
auth_storage.sync_store_id_mismatch: {provider} holds a different vault than this device. Connect from Settings to choose which copy to keep.
auth_storage.sync_conflict_store_id_banner: Different vault on {provider} — choose which copy to keep.
auth_storage.sync_conflict_imported_vault: Imported vault from {provider}. Unlock it from the vault picker.
auth_storage.security_conflict_edits: Security conflict detected. Sync from all devices before editing.
auth_storage.sync_blocked_edits: Resolve the sync conflict before changing secrets.
auth_storage.duplicate_sync_provider: This sync provider is already connected with the same storage target.
auth_storage.local_folder_choose_err: Choose a local backup folder before connecting.
auth_storage.local_folder_multiple_vaults_short: Choose a folder with one vault backup before syncing.
auth_storage.local_folder_multiple_vaults_desc: {provider} points at a folder that contains more than one vault backup.
auth_storage.local_folder_multiple_vaults_resolution: Use a dedicated folder for one vault. Pick another folder, or disconnect this provider and organize the backup files outside Nook.

pending_joins.instructions: Join requests sync from your cloud providers into the local vault. Approve here or in Settings → Devices.

add_secret.seed_wordlist_error: Could not load the official BIP39 wordlist.

toasts.device_approved: Your device was approved. Click Connect vault.
toasts.device_removed: This device was removed from the vault.
toasts.enrolled_connected: Enrolled and connected to the vault.
toasts.github_connected: Connected to GitHub. Encryption key is stored locally in this browser.
toasts.google_drive_connected: Connected to Google Drive. Encryption key is stored locally in this browser.
toasts.device_enrolled: Enrolled this device via enrollment code.
toasts.error_timeout: Connection timed out. Check your PAT, network, and try again.
toasts.error_unlock_required: Unlock the vault before adding a password.
toasts.password_added_rotate: Added password. Please rotate the genesis password envelope on your other devices if you intend to unlock them with this password.

login_wizard.connection_desc: Connect to storage, then get access.
login_wizard.unlock_desc: Enter vault password or scan enrollment QR.
login_wizard.no_providers: No providers saved. Set up one below.

login_wizard.confirm_enroll_desc: Confirm password to finalize enrollment and generate keys.
login_wizard.enroll_warning: Wait, this device is not yet enrolled.
login_wizard.enroll_choose: Choose a way to enroll it:
login_wizard.enroll_scan_qr: Scan enrollment QR code from an active device
login_wizard.connection_subheader: Pick where your vault file lives
login_wizard.choose_store_vault: Choose where to store your vault
login_wizard.add_provider_desc: Pick another place for an encrypted vault file. Each saved provider is a separate vault location in this browser.
login_wizard.before_connect_desc: Before you can connect, pick a sync provider — Google Drive, iCloud, or GitHub.
login_wizard.manage_providers_desc: Add or remove saved providers. Vault files on storage are not deleted.

remote_recovery.title: Vault file missing on storage
remote_recovery.desc_with_cache: The encrypted vault file is no longer on your remote storage, but this browser still has a local copy from a previous session. Recover it to recreate the file on storage.
remote_recovery.desc_missing_only: The encrypted vault file is no longer on your remote storage, and this browser has no local copy to restore. You can create a new vault file from scratch — this replaces what was there before.

join_enrollment.desc_join: This browser is not enrolled yet. Device keys (not your master password) link this browser to the vault roster.
join_enrollment.desc_pending: Your join request was sent to the vault copy on the sync provider. Try unlocking again after an enrolled device approves you.
join_enrollment.explainer_item1: Join uses device keys — your master password unlocks secrets after enrollment, but cannot replace the approval step.
join_enrollment.explainer_item2: Send a request; an enrolled device approves it under Vault → Settings → Devices.
join_enrollment.explainer_item3: Approval wraps the vault keys for this browser’s public key — no plaintext secrets are shared.
join_enrollment.explainer_item4: Approved roster changes fan out to every connected sync provider automatically.
join_enrollment.have_transfer_keys: Have transfer keys from another device?
join_enrollment.transfer_keys_desc: Paste the two keys copied from an enrolled device. This skips the approval step — only use if you already received them out of band.
join_enrollment.setup_from_scratch_desc: If you cleared this browser and remote storage, create a new vault here. This replaces any existing vault file with a fresh one for this browser.
join_enrollment.approve_on_enrolled: Open Nook on an enrolled device, approve this browser in Vault settings, then unlock again here.
join_enrollment.starting_over_desc: Create a new vault if you reset storage and no longer have an enrolled device to approve this browser.

product_intro.device_is_key: Your vault lives on this device
product_intro.no_master_pw: Encrypted locally with your master password. Optional cloud sync after unlock.
product_intro.bullet2: Your secrets. Your storage. Your keys.
product_intro.bullet3: A decentralized vault for your secrets.

provider_picker.this_device_desc: Encrypted vault in browser storage — no provider login
provider_picker.local_folder_desc: Choose a folder — backup encrypted event files on this computer
provider_picker.local_folder_unavailable_desc: Requires a browser with writable folder access
provider_picker.unsupported_replication_desc: This provider does not support the selected replication mode.
provider_picker.unsupported_current_vault: Unavailable for this vault's replication mode

provider_picker.google_drive_desc: Sign in with Google — sync encrypted vault to your Drive app data
provider_picker.icloud_desc: Sign in with Apple — sync encrypted vault to your iCloud private database

provider_setup.github_pat_desc: Nook needs a classic personal access token (ghp_) with repo scope to sync encrypted vault events.
provider_setup.github_connection_subtitle: Create a token and choose your repository
provider_setup.event_log_desc: Event log: nook-log/v1/events in this repo under your account.
provider_setup.drive_event_log_desc: Event log records are stored as encrypted YAML backups in your Google Drive app data folder.
provider_setup.stored_locally_desc: Stored in this browser only. Syncs events to username/{repo}/nook-log/v1/events.
provider_setup.local_storage_desc: Your vault is stored in IndexedDB on this device. No token needed.
provider_setup.local_folder_desc: Nook writes encrypted event files to nook-log/v1/events in the folder you choose.
provider_setup.local_folder_connection_subtitle: Grant write access to a dedicated folder
provider_setup.local_folder_unsupported_browser: Local backup needs a browser that can grant write access to a folder.
provider_setup.local_folder_sync_subtitle: Merge events and write missing backup files
provider_setup.google_drive_desc: Nook stores your encrypted vault in a hidden Google Drive app folder. Click Sign in with Google — no API keys to copy.
provider_setup.google_connection_subtitle: Sign in with Google and choose your vault file
provider_setup.google_sync_subtitle: Save this provider and reconcile with your vault
provider_setup.google_tokens_local_desc: OAuth tokens stay in this browser only, like a saved GitHub token.
provider_setup.google_oauth_unconfigured: Google sign-in is not configured. Set GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID in google-oauth-config.ts.
provider_setup.oauth_preview_origin_unsupported: OAuth sign-in is disabled on PR preview origin {origin}. Google and Apple require exact registered origins; use production or local dev to test this provider.
provider_setup.oauth_origin_unsupported: OAuth sign-in is not enabled for {origin}. Add this exact origin to the provider console before testing this provider here.
provider_setup.icloud_desc: Nook stores your encrypted vault as a private CloudKit record in your iCloud account. Click Sign in with Apple — no API keys to copy.
provider_setup.icloud_connection_subtitle: Sign in with Apple and choose your vault file
provider_setup.icloud_sync_subtitle: Save this provider and reconcile with your vault
provider_setup.icloud_tokens_local_desc: CloudKit web auth tokens stay in this browser only, like a saved GitHub token.
provider_setup.icloud_event_log_desc: Event log records are stored as encrypted YAML backups in your iCloud private database.
provider_setup.icloud_oauth_unconfigured: iCloud sign-in is not configured. Set ICLOUD_CONTAINER_ID and ICLOUD_API_TOKEN in icloud-oauth-config.ts.

devices_card.single_device_warning: Add another device before removing this one. A backup password helps with recovery, but at least one device key must remain enrolled.
devices_card.nexus_single_device_warning: Add enough participating devices to satisfy this Nexus vault's threshold before changing its membership.
devices_card.nexus_device_change_guidance: Nexus membership changes require an atomic share-rotation ceremony. Direct device revocation is unavailable until that ceremony is implemented.
devices_card.no_devices: No enrolled devices found for this vault.
devices_card.confirm_revoke_current: Remove this browser from the vault and lock it?
devices_card.confirm_revoke_other: Remove this device from future vault access?

manifest.name: Nook Passwords
manifest.description: Nook browser companion for password form detection and future autofill.

setup.passkey_device_description: Protect this browser profile with a passkey-backed extension device before connecting Nook.
